input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. Use `DynamicResource`, not `StaticResource`**

`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time and never updates. Replace with:

```xml
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime by merging a new dictionary:

```csharp
var mergedDictionaries = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDictionaries.Clear();
mergedDictionaries.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Prevent Activity restart on UI mode change**

Android destroys/recreates the Activity on configuration changes like dark mode. In `MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme", MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                  | ConfigChanges.UiMode | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                  | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the UI mode change itself, so it won't restart. You can then detect the system change and swap your `ResourceDictionary` accordingly by overriding `OnConfigurationChanged` or listening to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but when they switch themes at runtime, nothing changes.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode setting changes** — When the user toggles dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts completely.

These are common .NET MAUI/Android issues I can answer directly without needing to look at any code.

**Issue 1:** `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which listens for changes to the resource dictionary and updates the UI automatically.
 
**Issue 2:** Android destroys and recreates the Activity when the UI mode changes by default. They can prevent this by adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to the Activity attribute in `MainActivity.cs` and handling the configuration change manually instead of letting the system restart the app.
